name: Cubillas Reservoir Loop from Albolote
name_original: Pantano de Cubillas — circular desde Albolote
description: This moderate 15.2-mile mountain bike route circles the scenic Cubillas Reservoir near Albolote, offering a good workout.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Albolote offers diverse landscapes, from the rugged Sierra Elvira mountain range to the tranquil surroundings of the Cubillas Reservoir. The region features varied terrain, including technical trails with loose rock and steep descents, as well as flowing sections through verdant pine forests. Geological formations like dolomites and limestones characterize the area, providing panoramic views of the Vega de Granada. Ancient footpaths and a "badlands look" with red rock add to the unique riding experience. Best mountain bike trails around Albolote The most popular mountain bike trail is Cubillas Reservoir Loop from Albolote, a 15.2 miles (24.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Plaza Nueva, Granada – Sundial of Llano de la Perdiz loop from Juncaril, a moderate 27.4 miles (44.1 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain, including sections with significant elevation gain. Local mountain bikers also love the Cubillas Reservoir – Cubillas Reservoir loop from Albolote, a 32.4 miles (52.1 km) trail leading through the reservoir's natural environment, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Mountain biking around Albolote is defined by the Sierra Elvira mountain range, the Cubillas Reservoir, and diverse geological formations. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy trails to challenging enduro experiences. The routes in Albolote are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 8,000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Albolote's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Albolote?
A: There are 17 mountain bike trails around Albolote. These include 2 easy, 10 moderate, and 5 difficult routes, offering options for various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Albolote?
A: The trails around Albolote offer diverse terrain, from the rugged Sierra Elvira with technical sections, loose rock, and steep descents, to more flowing paths through pine forests. You'll encounter varied geological formations, including dolomites and limestones, and some areas even feature a "badlands look" with red rock. Many routes also follow ancient footpaths, some with historical significance.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Albolote?
A: Yes, Albolote caters to all levels. There are 2 easy trails with minimal elevation gain, suitable for beginners or families looking for a relaxed ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016781058/cubillas-reservoir-loop-from-albolote" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubillas Reservoir Loop from Albolote&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that offers scenic views and is a good option for those building their skills.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 5 difficult routes around Albolote. The Sierra Elvira mountain range offers technical trails with steep descents and demanding sections, some even requiring you to push your bike. Specific challenging sections like "Raja Santa" are known for their verticality and dry terrain, providing pure Enduro experiences.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Albolote?
A: Yes, many routes around Albolote are circular. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10304233" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubillas Reservoir – Cubillas Reservoir loop from Albolote&lt;/a&gt; is a 32.4 miles (52.1 km) trail that takes you through the natural environment surrounding the reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40361230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Nueva, Granada – Sundial of Llano de la Perdiz loop from Juncaril&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 27.4 miles (44.1 km) with significant elevation gain.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer views of significant landmarks and natural features. The 12th-century Arab watchtower, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6499477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreón de Albolote&lt;/a&gt;, is a frequent point of interest, offering panoramic views. You can also ride around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1627469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubillas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5511812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt;. The region's diverse geology, including dolomites and limestones, also provides stunning scenery.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about riding in Albolote?
A: The mountain bike trails around Albolote are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 800 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, from the rugged Sierra Elvira to the tranquil Cubillas Reservoir, and the variety of terrain that caters to all skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Albolote?
A: The region's climate makes it suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures. While winter riding is possible, it's advisable to check local weather conditions, especially for higher elevations in Sierra Elvira, as temperatures can drop.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra Elvira mountain range provides stunning panoramic views of the Vega de Granada. Routes that include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6499477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreón de Albolote&lt;/a&gt; are particularly good for viewpoints, offering expansive vistas of the surrounding area and plains below.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trailheads in Albolote?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Albolote, particularly near common starting points for trails. Many routes, such as those starting from Albolote or Juncaril, have accessible parking options for cyclists.

Q: Can I find accommodation in Albolote that is suitable for cyclists?
A: Albolote, being a popular area for outdoor activities, offers various accommodation options. Many local hotels and guesthouses are accustomed to hosting cyclists and may provide facilities like secure bike storage. It's always a good idea to inquire about specific cyclist-friendly amenities when booking.
tips: Large esplanade where you can take a breather and enjoy the fantastic views of the swamp. It is perfectly accessible by bicycle.

In a deep valley between Sierra Elvira, the mountains of Colomera and Sierra Arana, the reservoir was built in 1956. Its purpose is to supply the surrounding towns and irrigate the fields with water from the Cubillas River.
From this same point you can see impressive views of the Sierra Nevada.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route starts conveniently in Albolote, right next to a parking lot, making access straightforward for mountain bikers.

faqs.question: How difficult is this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. It's a 24.5 km circular trail with about 310 meters of elevation gain. Riders should have good fitness and advanced riding skills, as it may include challenging sections comparable to STS S2 on the Single Track Scale.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this circular route?
faqs.answer: While specific details for this exact loop are not fully detailed, mountain biking trails in the Granada region often feature rocky technical sections and loose terrain. You can expect scenic views as the trail circles the Embalse del Cubillas (Cubillas Reservoir).

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: On average, it takes about 1 hour and 51 minutes to complete the full 24.5 km circular route.

faqs.question: What can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers pleasant surroundings and scenic views as it circles the Cubillas Reservoir. You'll also pass along the Shore of the Cubillas Reservoir, providing beautiful natural landscapes.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify if dogs are allowed or if there are any specific leash rules for this particular mountain bike route. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time of year to ride this trail is not explicitly stated. However, considering its location in Granada, spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures and more pleasant riding conditions than the heat of summer or the cooler, potentially wetter winter months.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access and ride this mountain bike route around the Pantano de Cubillas.

faqs.question: Is this a family-friendly mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating, which suggests challenging sections and advanced riding skills, this route might not be suitable for all families, especially those with young children or beginner riders. It's more geared towards experienced mountain bikers.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it includes sections of the Camino de la Cuesta del Canal, Cañada de Calicasas-Cogollos, and Camino de Iznalloz, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any specific considerations for mountain biking around the Cubillas Reservoir?
faqs.answer: The circular nature of the route and its proximity to Albolote make it a practical option for a dedicated mountain biking outing. Be prepared for potentially rocky or loose terrain, and ensure you have the necessary skills for a moderate-rated trail.
